title: wahlenbergia vinciflora var. littoralis
intro: Wahlenbergia vinciflora var. littoralis, also known as the coastal bluebells or sea bluebells, is a variety of flowering plant native to the coastal areas of New Zealand. This plant is used in traditional medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Wahlenbergia vinciflora var. littoralis include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
